9. Description: A community-driven collectibles project featuring art by Burnt Toast. Doodles come in a joyful range of colors, traits and sizes with a collection size of 10,000. Each Doodle allows its owner to vote for experiences and activations paid for by the Doodles Community Treasury.
Member 1 - Introduction: Dave Broome has been in the Hollywood scene for nearly 25 years as a mainstream TV/Film producer. In 2002, he launched 25/7 Productions, bringing to life projects like NBC’s The Biggest Loser, Netflix’s Ultimate Beastmaster, and Halftime, a Jennifer Lopez documentary.
Member 2 - Title: Co-founder and Chief Creative Officer, Orange Comet
Member 2 - Introduction: Dante Ferrarini is an intrepid creator with over 20 years of experience working with globally-renowned brands such as Netflix, Marvel, Vanity Fair, Vogue, and countless others. He is the visionary force spearheading Orange Comet’s 3D and design efforts.
Member 3 - Introduction: Power of Women is an NFT movement of bold, diverse and empowering artworks by the award-winning illustrator, Leah Ibrahim Sams. Their mission is to use art to inspire, empower and enable future generations of women, girls and non-binary people around the world.
Member 4 - Introduction: Manchester City FC is an English Premier League club initially founded in 1880 as St Mark’s West Gorton. It officially became Manchester City FC in 1894 and has since then gone on to win the European Cup Winners’ Cup, seven League Championship titles, including six Premier League titles (2012, 2014, 2018, 2019, 2021, 2022), and six FA Cups.
Member 5 - Introduction: Patrick Moxey is a veteran music executive and founder of Helix Records. He previously founded Payday Records, the hip-hop label which signed Jay-Z first record, and Ultra Records, which dominated the dance music industry for years.

9. Description:
"We really intend to open a whole new door for you, the fan, with these NFTs that goes far beyond the door that was accessible through the traditional record store" - Patrick Moxey

The Genesis holders become an integral part of Helix Records and form the backbone of the label. Together, they have the opportunity to work with Patrick Moxey and his team to make Helix Records the biggest EDM label in the world. Just like Patrick did with his old company Ultra Records, where he played a key role in the success of Avicii, Kygo, deadmau5 and David Guetta. Along with the Genesis holders, Helix aims to produce the next superstars. 

In addition, Genesis Pass holders will have access to exclusive digital and real-world utilities and will be rewarded with ongoing and almost unlimited opportunities to create unique and incredible experiences. Musicians gain access to the professional music world: access to world-class recording studios, track submission and direct contact with top A&Rs and more.
Member 1 - Title: Sound engineer, studio manager, A&R for Helix Records and Ultra Publishing
Member 1 - Introduction: Ben Sorab is the sound engineer and studio manager responsible for Helix Records’ European recording studios. He also works as an A&R for Ultra Publishing. He has recorded and worked with Central Cee, Sir Tom Jones, Armin Van Buuren and many more in addition to his regular work with the Helix Records and Ultra Publishing roster. An NFT enthusiast who has been collecting since 2020, Ben has been providing exclusive studio content for fans on our Discord server.
Member 2 - Introduction: Helix Records is the new flagship electronic music label by veteran music executive Patrick Moxey. With Ultra Records, which he founded in 1995 and sold to Sony Music earlier this year, Moxey has had massive successes, bringing superstars like Steve Aoki, Kygo, Lost Frequencies, Deorro, DVBBS, Benny Benassi, Headhunterz, Era Istrefi and many more to the very top, while featuring releases of huge artists like Avicii, deadmau5, Alan Walker, Tiësto, Armin van Buuren, David Guetta & Pitbull, just to name a few. He also entered a global alliance with Ultra Music Festival, which a few years later was named world’s top festival by renowned music magazine DJ Mag. And did we mention that he also founded Payday records, the hip-hop label that launched the career of Jay-Z and many others?
